title: pittosporum glabratum var. neriifolium
intro: Pittosporum glabratum var. neriifolium, also known as the 'black sheoak' or 'black ironwood', is a shrub or small tree native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its dark, glossy foliage. The leaves and bark of the Pittosporum glabratum var. neriifolium plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and bark have been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Pittosporum glabratum var. neriifolium are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
